input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:

completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

## 1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime

`StaticResource` resolves **once** at load time. To react to theme changes, use **`DynamicResource`**:

```xml
<!-- ✗ Won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ Updates when the dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap dictionaries at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme());   // or LightTheme
```

## 2. Activity restarts on Android config change

Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when the system UI mode changes. Tell it to handle the change itself in **`Platforms/Android/MainActivity.cs`**:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    // Add UiMode to the existing ConfigurationChanges flags:
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

The key addition is **`ConfigChanges.UiMode`** — it prevents the full restart and lets your app handle the dark/light switch in-process, where your `DynamicResource` bindings will pick up the new theme.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two .NET MAUI issues related to dark mode theming on Android:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors from ResourceDictionary themes, but when they switch themes at runtime, nothing changes. This is because `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead, which listens for changes and updates automatically.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user toggles dark mode in Android system settings, the app restarts completely. This is because Android recreates the Activity by default when the UI mode (dark/light) changes. They need to handle this configuration change in their Android `MainActivity` by adding `ConfigChanges` flags.


I should provide clear, actionable solutions for both issues—showing them how to switch to `DynamicResource` in their XAML and how to configure the `MainActivity` to prevent the restart when system dark mode changes.
